name: Lytx DriveCam Fleet Safety API
what_it_does: Lytx REST API for video telematics and fleet safety platform (DriveCam). Enables AI agents to manage dashcam event and video retrieval workflows, handle risky driving behavior event data (hard braking, speeding, distraction), access driver safety scoring and coaching data, retrieve fleet risk analytics and benchmarking, manage video-based incident investigation workflows, handle collision reconstruction and claims data, access driver coaching program management, retrieve compliance and hours-of-service data, manage fleet maintenance and vehicle health data, and integrate safety event data with TMS and insurance systems.
use_cases: Managing dashcam events and risky driving detection from fleet safety automation agents
Accessing driver safety scoring and coaching from safety program management agents
Retrieving incident video for claims investigation from fleet risk management agents
Integrating safety data with TMS and insurance from fleet safety analytics agents
not_for: Fleets without dashcam and video telematics investment
Consumer dashcam without commercial fleet management needs
Small fleets without driver coaching program infrastructure
best_when: A commercial fleet operator using Lytx DriveCam wants AI agents to automate driver safety monitoring, risky event detection, driver coaching workflows, incident investigation, and safety analytics.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Video dashcam footage of drivers and surroundings is highly sensitive — employee surveillance regulations vary by state (California, Illinois). Driver coaching automation must be applied fairly. Video data retention and access requires strict governance. Facial recognition from dashcam footage may be restricted.

auth.notes: Lytx uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. Fleet-level credentials with safety events, video, and analytics scopes. Developer portal with API documentation. Webhooks for safety event notifications. Partnerships with insurance carriers for risk-based pricing programs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-vehicle subscription with hardware required
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-vehicle per-month subscription; hardware (DriveCam device) plus platform
pricing.notes: San Diego, California. Founded 2001 (originally DriveCam). Pioneer in video-based fleet safety. 1M+ devices deployed globally. Owned by private equity (Permira). Used by trucking, transit, construction, and government fleets. Machine learning-based risky driving detection. Competes with Samsara and Netradyne for video telematics.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; video retrieval throttled

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Dashcam video of drivers is employee surveillance — varies by state (CA, IL); requires written notice
Video retrieval is bandwidth-intensive — large video files require async download workflows
No public MCP server — REST API with developer portal
Driver coaching automation must apply consistently to avoid discrimination claims
Hardware dependency — API requires physical DriveCam device installed in vehicles
Insurance integration data-sharing requires driver consent and carrier-specific agreements
agent_readiness.security_notes: Video telematics and driver footage. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU/AU data residency. Employee video surveillance sensitivity.

security_score_components.security_notes: Driver dashcam video and safety data. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU/AU data residency. Employee surveillance regulations.
